task_completion.description: Verify that the document margins have been adjusted according to the rules.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Page Style/Page Setup dialog box showing modified margin values, or observe a visible shift in the document's text boundaries relative to the ruler.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required header text has been added to the document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the top margin (header area) of the document pages for the exact text 'THP Name: Bear Creek Harvest'.

task_completion.description: Verify that H1 and H2 sections have been formatted with proper heading styles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for section titles like 'General Information' (H1) and 'Site Description' (H2) to be formatted distinctly from body text (e.g., larger font, bolded, or using built-in 'Heading 1' / 'Heading 2' styles visible in the style dropdown).

task_completion.description: Verify that the watercourse data has been converted into a table.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a structured grid/table containing the watercourse data, replacing what was previously plain text or tab-separated values.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Table of Contents has been generated.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Table of Contents block, typically at the beginning of the document, listing the H1 and H2 headings along with their corresponding page numbers.

task_completion.description: Verify that scientific/Latin names have been properly styled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Latin species names (e.g., 'Oncorhynchus kisutch', 'Sequoia sempervirens') in the text. They should be formatted with a distinct scientific style, which is typically italics.

task_completion.description: Verify that the formatted document has been saved to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the save action being completed, such as the title bar showing 'bear_creek_thp.odt' without an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk), or a 'Save As' dialog confirming the path '/home/ga/Documents/bear_creek_thp.odt'.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used a graphical word processor (like LibreOffice Writer) to format the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the LibreOffice Writer interface (or similar GUI) being actively used, rather than terminal commands or Python scripts modifying the .odt file directly.

integrity.description: Ensure the Table of Contents was generated by the word processor's built-in tool, not manually typed.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Table of Contents should look like an automated field (often indicated by a light grey background behind the text in LibreOffice) rather than plain text manually aligned with dots and numbers.

integrity.description: Ensure the watercourse data is in a genuine table.
integrity.visual_evidence: The watercourse data should be enclosed in actual table cells with selectable gridlines or table properties visible, not just text aligned using spaces or tabs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The document must be saved to '/home/ga/Documents/bear_creek_thp.odt'. The header text must exactly match 'THP Name: Bear Creek Harvest'. Expected H1 sections to be styled: General Information, Silviculture, Harvesting Practices, Erosion Control, Biological Resources, Alternatives. Expected H2 subsections to be styled: Site Description, Stand Characteristics, Silvicultural Methods, Yarding Methods, Winter Operations, Protected Species, Watercourse Protection, No Project Alternative. Latin names requiring scientific styling (typically italics): Oncorhynchus kisutch, Strix occidentalis caurina, Pekania pennanti, Rana boylii, Sequoia sempervirens.
